revision.text:
{{Short description|Archeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | native_name = 曲崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Wikidatacoord|Q28684169|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagasaki]], Japan | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th - 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|45|5|N|129|59|20|E}}}} | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> }}
The {{nihongo|'''Magarizaki Kofun Cluster'''|曲崎古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[burial mound]]s, located in the Makishima neighborhood of the city of [[Nagasaki, Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |title=曲崎古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=December 20, 2023 |archive-date=June 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240630042149/https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Overview== Makishima Island is located 500 meters offshore Tachibana Bay of the [[Amakusa Sea]], between the Nagasaki Peninsula and the [[Shimabara Peninsula]]. At its eastern end is a beak-shaped gravel bar called "Magarizaki", which in 1940 was discovered to contain a group of tombs is a group of piled stone mounds. These [[burial mound]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 burial mounds have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The burial mounds are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] and [[Saga Prefecture]]s. [[Grave goods]] including glass beads, jars, and [[Sue ware]] pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these burial mounds is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from [[Nagasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nagasaki Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/16 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.nagasaki.lg.jp/shimin/190001/192001/p000539.html Nagasaki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Archeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | native_name = 曲崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Wikidatacoord|Q28684169|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nagasaki]], Japan | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th - 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|45|5|N|129|59|20|E}}}} | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> }}
The {{nihongo|'''Magarizaki Kofun Cluster'''|曲崎古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]]s, located in the Makishima neighborhood of the city of [[Nagasaki, Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], [[Nagasaki Prefecture]] Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |title=曲崎古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=December 20, 2023 |archive-date=June 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240630042149/https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Overview== Makishima Island is located 500 meters offshore Tachibana Bay of the [[Amakusa Sea]], between the Nagasaki Peninsula and the [[Shimabara Peninsula]]. At its eastern end is a beak-shaped gravel bar called "Magarizaki", which in 1940 was discovered to contain a group of tombs is a group of piled stone mounds. These [[Kofun]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 Kofuns have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The Kofuns are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in [[Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] and [[Saga Prefecture]]s. [[Grave goods]] including glass beads, jars, and [[Sue pottery]] pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from [[Nagasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nagasaki Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/16 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.nagasaki.lg.jp/shimin/190001/192001/p000539.html Nagasaki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Magarizaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:曲崎古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28684169]] Wikidata name: Magarizaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Archeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | native_name = 曲崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Wikidatacoord|Q28684169|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|32.75138888888889|N|129.98888888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], Japan | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th - 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|45|5|N|129|59|20|E}}}} | website = }}
The {{nihongo|'''Magarizaki Kofun Cluster'''|曲崎古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]]s, located in the Makishima neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |title=曲崎古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=December 20, 2023 |archive-date=June 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240630042149/https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Overview== Makishima Island is located 500 meters offshore Tachibana Bay of the [[:en:Amakusa Sea|Amakusa Sea]], between the Nagasaki Peninsula and the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]]. At its eastern end is a beak-shaped gravel bar called "Magarizaki", which in 1940 was discovered to contain a group of tombs is a group of piled stone mounds. These [[Kofun]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 Kofuns have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The Kofuns are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] and [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]s. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] including glass beads, jars, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from [[:en:Nagasaki Station|Nagasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nagasaki Main Line|Nagasaki Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/16 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.nagasaki.lg.jp/shimin/190001/192001/p000539.html Nagasaki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Magarizaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:曲崎古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28684169]] Wikidata name: Magarizaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Archeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | native_name = 曲崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Wikidatacoord|Q28684169|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|32.75138888888889|N|129.98888888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], Japan | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th - 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|45|5|N|129|59|20|E}}}} | website = }}
The {{nihongo|'''Magarizaki Kofun Cluster'''|曲崎古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]]s, located in the Makishima neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |title=曲崎古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=December 20, 2023 |archive-date=June 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240630042149/https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Overview== Makishima Island is located 500 meters offshore Tachibana Bay of the [[:en:Amakusa Sea|Amakusa Sea]], between the Nagasaki Peninsula and the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]]. At its eastern end is a beak-shaped gravel bar called "Magarizaki", which in 1940 was discovered to contain a group of tombs is a group of piled stone mounds. These [[Kofun]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 Kofuns have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The Kofuns are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] and [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]s. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] including glass beads, jars, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from [[:en:Nagasaki Station|Nagasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nagasaki Main Line|Nagasaki Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Magarizaki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:曲崎古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28684169]] Wikidata name: Magarizaki Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Archeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | native_name = 曲崎古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Nagasaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magarizaki Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Wikidatacoord|Q28684169|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|32.75138888888889|N|129.98888888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], Japan | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th - 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|45|5|N|129|59|20|E}}}} | website = }}
The {{nihongo|'''Magarizaki Kofun Cluster'''|曲崎古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]]s, located in the Makishima neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagasaki|Nagasaki]], [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |title=曲崎古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=December 20, 2023 |archive-date=June 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240630042149/https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Overview== Makishima Island is located 500 meters offshore Tachibana Bay of the [[:en:Amakusa Sea|Amakusa Sea]], between the Nagasaki Peninsula and the [[:en:Shimabara Peninsula|Shimabara Peninsula]]. At its eastern end is a beak-shaped gravel bar called "Magarizaki", which in 1940 was discovered to contain a group of tombs is a group of piled stone mounds. These [[Kofun]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 Kofuns have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The Kofuns are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka]] and [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]s. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] including glass beads, jars, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from [[:en:Nagasaki Station|Nagasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nagasaki Main Line|Nagasaki Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/16 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.nagasaki.lg.jp/shimin/190001/192001/p000539.html Nagasaki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] including glass beads, jars, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from [[:en:Nagasaki Station|Nagasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nagasaki Main Line|Nagasaki Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Magarizaki Kofun Cluster'''|曲崎古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]]s, located in the Makishima neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Nagasaki|en|Nagasaki|WD=Q38234|lt=Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}} Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |title=曲崎古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=December 20, 2023 |archive-date=June 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240630042149/https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Overview== Makishima Island is located 500 meters offshore Tachibana Bay of the {{ill|Amakusa Sea|en|Amakusa Sea|WD=Q3306188|lt=Amakusa Sea}}, between the Nagasaki Peninsula and the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula}}. At its eastern end is a beak-shaped gravel bar called "Magarizaki", which in 1940 was discovered to contain a group of tombs is a group of piled stone mounds. These [[Kofun]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 Kofuns have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The Kofuns are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka}} and {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}s. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} including glass beads, jars, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from {{ill|Nagasaki Station|en|Nagasaki Station|WD=Q1076315|lt=Nagasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|en|Nagasaki Main Line|WD=Q1078117|lt=Nagasaki Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/16 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.nagasaki.lg.jp/shimin/190001/192001/p000539.html Nagasaki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Magarizaki Kofun Cluster'''|曲崎古墳群|}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]]s, located in the Makishima neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Nagasaki|en|Nagasaki|WD=Q38234|lt=Nagasaki}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|WD=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture}} Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |title=曲崎古墳群 |language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=December 20, 2023 |archive-date=June 30, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240630042149/https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211490 |url-status=live }}</ref>
==Overview== Makishima Island is located 500 meters offshore Tachibana Bay of the {{ill|Amakusa Sea|en|Amakusa Sea|WD=Q3306188|lt=Amakusa Sea}}, between the Nagasaki Peninsula and the {{ill|Shimabara Peninsula|en|Shimabara Peninsula|WD=Q522605|lt=Shimabara Peninsula}}. At its eastern end is a beak-shaped gravel bar called "Magarizaki", which in 1940 was discovered to contain a group of tombs is a group of piled stone mounds. These [[Kofun]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 Kofuns have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The Kofuns are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka}} and {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}s. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} including glass beads, jars, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from {{ill|Nagasaki Station|en|Nagasaki Station|WD=Q1076315|lt=Nagasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|en|Nagasaki Main Line|WD=Q1078117|lt=Nagasaki Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

These [[Kofun]]s extend over an area 400 meters north-to-south by 50 meters east-to-west, at an elevation of four meters above sea level. Thus far, 101 Kofuns have been confirmed, and there are also about 500 more mounds whose nature is unknown. The Kofuns are small, with the largest being 15 meters in diameter, and most are even to eight meters in diameter or less. The main burial facilities are horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s constructed of slab-shaped stones in the lower half, and round pebbles and broken stones are piled up in the upper half. The presence of an passageway has not been fully confirmed, but it is possible that these tumuli are similar to the pit-type horizontal-entrance stone burial chambers found in other locations in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}s. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} including glass beads, jars, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pots have been excavated, from which the construction date of these Kofuns is estimated to be the late Kofun period, between the end of the 5th century and the beginning of the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is about a 37-minute drive from {{ill|Nagasaki Station|en|Nagasaki Station|WD=Q1076315|lt=Nagasaki Station|1=Nagasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|en|Nagasaki Main Line|WD=Q1078117|lt=Nagasaki Main Line|1=Nagasaki Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagasaki)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/16 Nagasaki Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.nagasaki.lg.jp/shimin/190001/192001/p000539.html Nagasaki City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
